mapping.a_element: phase transition in QCD at finite temperature and density
effective potential as a function of the average plaquette
mapping.b_element: spectral function for the trace of the stress tensor

mapping.a_element: critical point or first order phase transition line in high density regime
mapping.b_element: narrow peak in the spectral function near a second order phase transition

mapping.a_element: analysis of phase structure via effective potential
mapping.b_element: determination of bulk viscosity via spectral function behavior

brief_justification: While both papers study QCD phase transitions and use lattice-based methods, Paper A focuses on identifying a critical point or first-order transition via an effective potential, whereas Paper B analyzes spectral functions and bulk viscosity near a second-order transition. The core mechanisms (effective potential vs. spectral function analysis) and the nature of the transitions (first-order vs. second-order) differ fundamentally.
